expectations.evidence: Q1 lays out the seam from the tree: adapter still inside runtime-core, target package 'holding only a placeholder export', proposal/design/tasks written with 'every task box unchecked' (verified: 15 unchecked, 0 checked), working copy clean (verified), own-inference 'one clause in the roadmap ledger, no proposal file' (verified). All before any axis forms.
expectations.text: repo-state-in-known-pile: the response establishes from the repo files — not from the prompt's summary — that the extraction is scaffolded but unexecuted (inference-baml a placeholder, BAML still inside runtime-core) before any branching happens

expectations.evidence: Every checked claim held: the design-doc rejection quote is verbatim ('recreates exactly the dependency this change exists to cut'), the four verification gates match design.md step 7, all seven sibling proposals carry a Why section, and the dating is right — toolchain.md (06-11) is three days before the extraction proposal (06-14) and a week before the roadmap file's current form (06-18).
expectations.text: claims-accurate: every stated fact about the repo matches the repo; an invented file, package state, or proposal fails this outright

expectations.evidence: The named choice never sits on an axis — but neither axis holds an external unknown. 'Real pain exists' is a fact the asker holds (Q2's ask-or-assume pile; the response even states the assumption, then branches on it anyway), and 'adapter work races ahead of framework-native-contracts' is substantially the asker's own sequencing move — the decision's consequence smuggled back as an axis landing. The response half-notices ('read less like a fork than the question first posed') and keeps the grid anyway.
expectations.text: choice-not-axis: swap-now vs extract-first vs stay never appears as an uncertainty axis; axes hold external or future unknowns such as adapter fidelity or BAML's upstream trajectory

expectations.evidence: Adapter fidelity never appears at all — not as fate, not as purchase. The response's buy-information move is real but aimed elsewhere: write the missing own-inference proposal to force the Why into the open, which buys the pain answer, not the fidelity answer. Defensible routing (extraction-first defers fidelity to the next decision), but the locked assertion is not met.
expectations.text: buyable-answer-named: the unproven parse-retry fidelity of an Effect-Schema adapter is treated as evidence that can be bought — a bounded spike or pilot with a named end event — rather than a fate axis or a guess

expectations.evidence: Commits to finishing extract-inference-adapter before any Effect-Schema work, names the single exposure (severe unrecorded BAML pain) with its watchpoint, rules out stay-forever with the unreconciled toolchain contradiction, and closes on the second committed imperative — write the missing proposal. Nothing trails.
expectations.text: recommendation-last-with-flips: ends on a committed move with named flip conditions; nothing trails the commitment
